title: thevetia alliodora
intro: Thevetia alliodora, also known as the Yellow Oleander, is a plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its vibrant yellow flowers and toxic sap. The medicinal properties of Thevetia alliodora lie in its heart-shaped seeds and leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its potential use as a natural remedy for fever reduction, inflammation, and as a diuretic to treat urinary tract issues. The herbal preparations of Thevetia alliodora typically involve drying and grinding the seeds and leaves, which can be made into teas, capsules, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
